name: Chocolate
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, increased thirst, panting, restlessness, excessive urination, rapid heart rate, muscle spasms, seizures, death.
notes: Contains theobromine and caffeine. Dark chocolate, baking chocolate, and cocoa powder are highly toxic.

name: Grapes
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, dehydration, loss of appetite, decreased or absent urination.
notes: Can cause acute, irreversible kidney failure. Even a single grape or raisin can be fatal to some dogs.

name: Raisins
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, dehydration, loss of appetite, decreased or absent urination.
notes: Concentrated form of grapes. Highly toxic, causing acute kidney failure.

name: Onions
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, lethargy, pale gums, rapid heart rate, red-colored urine.
notes: Contains thiosulfates which cause oxidative damage to red blood cells, leading to hemolytic anemia. Cats are highly sensitive.

name: Garlic
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, lethargy, pale gums, rapid heart rate, red-colored urine.
notes: More potent than onions. Causes hemolytic anemia by damaging red blood cells.

name: Leeks
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, lethargy, pale gums, rapid heart rate, red-colored urine.
notes: Part of the Allium family. Causes damage to red blood cells.

name: Chives
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, lethargy, pale gums, rapid heart rate, red-colored urine.
notes: Part of the Allium family. Even dried or powdered forms are highly toxic.

name: Xylitol (Birch Sugar)
symptoms: Vomiting, weakness, loss of coordination, lethargy, tremors, seizures, yellowing of skin/gums, death.
notes: Common sweetener in sugar-free gum, peanut butter, and baked goods. Causes rapid, severe hypoglycemia and liver failure.

name: Lilies (Lilium species)
symptoms: Vomiting, drooling, lethargy, loss of appetite, dehydration, kidney failure.
notes: Ingestion of any part of the plant, including pollen or water from the vase, causes acute renal failure in cats.

name: Macadamia Nuts
symptoms: Weakness (especially in hind legs), depression, vomiting, tremors, high body temperature (hyperthermia).
notes: Symptoms usually resolve within 24-48 hours. Avoid all products containing macadamia nuts.

name: Caffeine
symptoms: Restlessness, hyperactivity, panting, vomiting, rapid or abnormal heart rate, tremors, seizures.
notes: Found in coffee, tea, energy drinks, and soda. Stimulates the central nervous and cardiovascular systems.

name: Yeast Dough
symptoms: Abdominal bloating, pain, retching, gas, weakness, loss of coordination, alcohol poisoning.
notes: Expands in the stomach causing painful bloating (GDV risk). Fermentation produces ethanol, causing alcohol poisoning.

name: Alcohol (Ethanol)
symptoms: Vomiting, disorientation, loss of coordination, slowed breathing, drop in body temperature, coma, seizures.
notes: Can cause severe central nervous system depression, low blood sugar, metabolic acidosis, and respiratory failure.

name: Avocado
symptoms: Vomiting and diarrhea in dogs/cats. Respiratory distress, fluid accumulation around heart in birds.
notes: Contains persin. Relatively mild for dogs/cats, but highly toxic to birds, rabbits, and livestock.

name: Macadamia Nuts
symptoms: Weakness, depression, vomiting, tremors, hyperthermia.
notes: Do not feed raw or roasted macadamia nuts.

name: Walnuts (Moldy)
symptoms: Tremors, seizures, vomiting, weakness, high fever.
notes: Can contain tremorgenic mycotoxins produced by molds, which are highly neurotoxic.

name: Pecans
symptoms: Upset stomach, vomiting, diarrhea, obstruction if shell is swallowed.
notes: High fat content can cause pancreatitis. Moldy pecans can contain tremorgenic mycotoxins.

name: Cherry Pits
symptoms: Dilated pupils, breathing difficulties, bright red gums, shock, weakness.
notes: Pits contain cyanide. Eating the fruit flesh is safe, but the pits are toxic and present a choking/obstruction hazard.

name: Peach Pits
symptoms: Dilated pupils, bright red gums, heavy breathing, shock, salivation.
notes: Contains cyanide. Also poses a major intestinal obstruction risk.

name: Plum Pits
symptoms: Breathing distress, dilated pupils, shock, brick-red mucous membranes.
notes: Pits contain amygdalin, which breaks down into cyanide.

name: Apple Seeds
symptoms: Dilated pupils, breathing difficulty, red gums, shock.
notes: Seeds contain amygdalin (cyanide compound). A few seeds won't hurt, but large quantities are toxic.

name: Apricot Pits
symptoms: Dilated pupils, breathing difficulty, shock, bright red gums.
notes: Contains cyanide compounds. Extremely hazardous if crushed or chewed.

name: Mustard Seeds
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, gastroenteritis.
notes: Contains compounds that irritate the stomach and intestinal lining.

name: Wild Mushrooms (Amanita species)
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, tear production, coordination loss, liver/kidney failure, seizures, death.
notes: Many wild mushrooms are highly toxic. Assume any wild mushroom is toxic unless identified by an expert.

name: Raw Potatoes
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, confusion, weakness.
notes: Contains solanine, a toxic glycoalkaloid. Cooking potatoes neutralizes solanine, making them safe.

name: Green Tomatoes
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, muscle weakness, confusion, slowed heart rate.
notes: Green parts of the tomato plant and unripe green fruits contain solanine. Ripe red tomatoes are safe in moderation.

name: Rhubarb Leaves
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, excessive drooling, tremors, blood in urine, kidney failure.
notes: Leaves contain soluble calcium oxalate crystals, which cause acute kidney injury.

name: Starfruit
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, salivation, blood in urine, tremors, kidney damage.
notes: Contains soluble calcium oxalates, which can cause acute kidney injury.

name: Hops (used in brewing)
symptoms: Malignant hyperthermia (extremely high body temp), panting, rapid heart rate, anxiety, vomiting, seizures.
notes: Both fresh and spent hops are dangerous. Can cause fatal hyperthermia within hours.

name: Nutmeg
symptoms: Dry mouth, abdominal pain, high heart rate, disorientation, tremors, seizures.
notes: Contains myristicin, which is hallucinogenic and toxic in large quantities.

name: Raw Meat & Fish
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, fever, lethargy, dehydration.
notes: Can carry Salmonella, E. coli, or Listeria. Raw fish can contain thiaminase, which causes thiamine (B1) deficiency.

name: Raw Eggs
symptoms: Skin and coat issues, lethargy, vomiting, diarrhea.
notes: Contains avidin, which inhibits biotin (Vitamin B7) absorption. Also carries Salmonella risk.

name: Citrus Oil / Peel
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, excessive drooling, skin irritation (if applied topically).
notes: Peels and essential oils contain limonene and linalool, which are toxic to the liver.

name: Salt (Sodium Chloride)
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, excessive thirst/urination, tremors, high fever, seizures, brain swelling, death.
notes: Eating large amounts of salt or salty foods leads to sodium ion poisoning (salt toxicity).

name: Macadamia Nuts
symptoms: Weakness, depression, vomiting, tremors, hyperthermia.
notes: High fat content also increases the risk of pancreatitis.

name: Dairy Products (Milk, Cheese)
symptoms: Diarrhea, gas, bloating, vomiting, abdominal discomfort.
notes: Most adult pets are lactose intolerant. Small amounts of plain cheese are usually tolerated, but large quantities cause upset.

name: Fat Trimmings
symptoms: Vomiting, severe abdominal pain, diarrhea, lethargy, pancreatitis.
notes: High fat meals trigger acute pancreatitis, which can be life-threatening.

name: Cooked Bones
symptoms: Choking, vomiting, blood in stool, constipation, lethargy, abdominal pain.
notes: Cooked bones splinter easily, causing throat lacerations, stomach perforation, or intestinal blockage.

name: Sago Palm
symptoms: Vomiting (often bloody), dark tarry stools, yellow skin/gums, bleeding disorders, liver failure, death.
notes: Contains cycasin. All parts are toxic, especially the seeds. Ingestion has a high mortality rate.

name: Azaleas (Rhododendron species)
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, weakness, depression, cardiovascular collapse, coma.
notes: Contains grayanotoxins, which disrupt skeletal and cardiac muscle function.

name: Tulip Bulbs
symptoms: Intense salivation, vomiting, diarrhea, depression, increased heart rate, difficulty breathing.
notes: The bulb contains the highest concentration of tulipanin toxins. Avoid digging up bulbs.

name: Oleander (Nerium oleander)
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, slow or irregular heart rate, drop in body temp, tremors, death.
notes: Contains cardiac glycosides. Extremely toxic; even a single leaf can be fatal.

name: Castor Bean
symptoms: Burning sensation in mouth, drooling, vomiting, severe bloody diarrhea, abdominal pain, seizures, death.
notes: Contains ricin, one of the most potent natural toxins known. Symptoms can take 12-48 hours to appear.

name: English Ivy
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, hyper-salivation.
notes: Contains triterpenoid saponins. Foliage is more toxic than the berries.

name: Chrysanthemums
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, coordination loss, skin inflammation (dermatitis).
notes: Contains pyrethrins, which irritate the gastrointestinal tract and nervous system.

name: Autumn Crocus
symptoms: Oral irritation, bloody vomiting, diarrhea, organ damage, bone marrow suppression, shock.
notes: Contains colchicine, causing multi-organ failure. Very different and far more toxic than Spring Crocus.

name: Poinsettias
symptoms: Drooling, mild vomiting, skin irritation.
notes: Often over-feared. Sap is irritating to the mouth and stomach, but rarely causes serious poisoning.

name: Mistletoe
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, low blood pressure, slowed heart rate, breathing difficulty.
notes: Contains toxalbumins and pharatoxin. Can cause cardiovascular collapse in large doses.

name: Holly Berries
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, lip-smacking, head-shaking.
notes: Leaves and berries contain saponins, which are gastrointestinal irritants.

name: Aspirin
symptoms: Vomiting (often bloody), dark tarry stools, rapid breathing, fever, coordination loss, kidney failure.
notes: NSAID medication. Cats are extremely sensitive due to a lack of liver enzymes to break it down.

name: Ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin)
symptoms: Vomiting, bloody stools, abdominal pain, weakness, kidney failure, stomach ulcers.
notes: Extremely toxic. Even one tablet can cause life-threatening stomach ulceration and renal failure.

name: Acetaminophen (Tylenol)
symptoms: Brown gums/tongue, swelling of face/paws, breathing difficulty, vomiting, dark urine, liver failure.
notes: Destroys red blood cells' ability to carry oxygen (methemoglobinemia). Cats are highly vulnerable.

name: Essential Oils (Tea Tree, Eucalyptus)
symptoms: Drooling, vomiting, tremors, coordination loss, weakness, low body temp, liver damage.
notes: Highly concentrated. Easily absorbed through skin or when licked. Keep out of reach.

name: Tobacco & Nicotine
symptoms: Hyperactivity, drooling, vomiting, rapid heart rate, tremors, collapse, seizures, death.
notes: Includes cigarettes, vape liquids, patches, and gums. Rapidly toxic.

name: Bleach
symptoms: Drooling, vomiting, chemical burns in mouth/throat, severe abdominal pain, coughing.
notes: Corrosive agent. Do not induce vomiting; contact a vet immediately.

name: Fertilizers
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, abnormal posture, breathing difficulty.
notes: Some contain bone/blood meal which makes them appealing to dogs, but they can contain toxic metals or pesticides.

name: Antifreeze (Ethylene Glycol)
symptoms: Initial 'drunken' behavior, vomiting, excessive thirst, rapid heart rate, kidney failure, coma, death.
notes: Has a sweet taste that pets like. Extremely toxic; causes crystallization in kidneys. Needs immediate treatment.

name: Rodenticides (Rat Poison)
symptoms: Internal bleeding, nosebleeds, bloody stool/urine, coughing blood, pale gums, weakness, seizures.
notes: Many types prevent blood clotting (anticoagulants) or cause brain swelling. Fatal without antidote (Vitamin K).

name: Slug and Snail Bait (Metaldehyde)
symptoms: Severe twitching, tremors, high fever, rapid breathing, anxiety, seizures, death.
notes: Known as 'shake and bake' syndrome due to extreme muscle spasms and life-threatening high body temp.

name: Macadamia Nuts
symptoms: Weakness, tremors, vomiting, joint pain.
notes: Keep all nut mixes away from dogs.

name: Persimmon Seeds
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, intestinal blockage.
notes: Seeds can cause inflammation or blockage in the small intestine.

name: Raw Salmon / Trout
symptoms: High fever, vomiting, bloody diarrhea, swollen lymph nodes, discharge from eyes/nose.
notes: Can carry Nanophyetus salmincola parasites infected with Neorickettsia helminthoeca bacteria (Salmon Poisoning Disease).

name: Xylitol (Toothpaste)
symptoms: Vomiting, low blood sugar, weakness, seizures.
notes: Never use human toothpaste on dogs; always use pet-safe toothpaste.

name: Unripe Tomatoes
symptoms: Lethargy, drooling, stomach upset, weakness.
notes: Solanine levels are high in green tomatoes and tomato vines.

name: Lilies of the Valley
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, low heart rate, severe cardiac arrhythmias, seizures.
notes: Contains cardendolides, which affect heart rate and rhythm.

name: Yew (Taxus species)
symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, weakness, breathing difficulty, heart rate drop, sudden death.
notes: Contains taxines. All parts of the plant are extremely poisonous. Can cause sudden cardiac failure.

name: Cyclamen
symptoms: Severe drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, heart arrhythmias, seizures, death.
notes: The tubers contain toxic cyclamin saponins, which are highly irritating.

name: Aloe Vera
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, tremors, change in urine color.
notes: Contains aloin, which acts as a laxative and irritates the GI tract.

name: Dieffenbachia (Dumb Cane)
symptoms: Intense oral burning, swelling of tongue/lips, difficulty swallowing, drooling.
notes: Contains insoluble calcium oxalate crystals which penetrate oral tissue, causing swelling.

name: Philodendron
symptoms: Oral pain, drooling, pawing at mouth, swelling, vomiting.
notes: Contains insoluble calcium oxalate crystals. Keep out of reach of pets.

name: Peace Lily
symptoms: Oral irritation, drooling, difficulty swallowing, vomiting.
notes: Unlike true lilies, it causes local irritation from calcium oxalate crystals, not systemic kidney failure.

name: Jade Plant
symptoms: Vomiting, depression, slowed heart rate (bradycardia), coordination loss.
notes: Toxic principle is unknown. Also known as rubber plant.

name: Snake Plant (Mother-in-Law's Tongue)
symptoms: Salivation, vomiting, diarrhea, depression.
notes: Contains saponins, which act as local gastrointestinal irritants.

name: Eucalyptus
symptoms: Salivation, vomiting, diarrhea, depression, weakness.
notes: Contains eucalyptol. Essential oil forms are significantly more toxic.

name: Marijuana (THC)
symptoms: Disorientation, dribbling urine, slowed heart rate, hyper-excitability, tremors, pupil dilation.
notes: Dogs have more cannabinoid receptors than humans, making them highly sensitive.

name: Mothballs
symptoms: Vomiting, pale/yellow gums, lethargy, seizures, anemia, kidney damage.
notes: Usually contain naphthalene or paradichlorobenzene. Highly toxic.

name: Cocoa Mulch
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, hyperactivity, rapid heart rate, seizures.
notes: Made from cocoa bean shells. Contains theobromine. Dogs are attracted to the chocolate smell.

name: Lead
symptoms: Vomiting, abdominal pain, behavior changes, blindness, coordination loss, seizures.
notes: From old paint chips, solder, or weights. Causes neurological and gastrointestinal toxicity.

name: Pennies (Minted post-1982)
symptoms: Vomiting, pale/yellow gums, red urine, lethargy, kidney failure, anemia.
notes: Post-1982 pennies are mostly zinc. Acid in stomach dissolves the coin, causing severe zinc poisoning.

name: Paint Thinner
symptoms: Drooling, difficulty breathing, vomiting, chemical burns, weakness, coma.
notes: Hydrocarbon solvent. Never induce vomiting, as aspiration into lungs is highly fatal.

name: Citrus Essential Oil
symptoms: Drooling, vomiting, tremors, low body temp, coordination loss.
notes: Keep diffusers out of areas where cats/dogs can inhale or lick the oil.

name: Onion Powder
symptoms: Vomiting, weakness, pale gums, red urine.
notes: Concentrated form of onions. Common in baby foods, gravies, and soups.

name: Garlic Powder
symptoms: Hemolytic anemia, pale gums, rapid heart rate, weakness.
notes: Concentrated form of garlic. Far more potent than fresh garlic cloves.

name: Solanine (Potato Sprouts)
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, lethargy, confusion.
notes: Always cut green parts and sprouts off potatoes before cooking for pets.

name: Solanine (Tomato Leaves)
symptoms: Vomiting, drooling, slowed heart rate, weakness.
notes: Keep pets away from tomato gardens.

name: Persin (Avocado Leaves)
symptoms: Congestion, breathing difficulty, fluid in lungs in birds.
notes: The leaves and bark contain the highest persin levels.

name: Cyanide (Apple Seeds)
symptoms: Breathing difficulty, red gums, dilated pupils, shock.
notes: Ensure apple slices are cored and deseeded before feeding to pets.

name: Lactose (Cow's Milk)
symptoms: Diarrhea, stomach gas, loose stools.
notes: Adult pets lack the lactase enzyme to digest cow's milk.

name: Tea Tree Oil
symptoms: Tremors, weakness, loss of coordination, low body temp, liver failure.
notes: Highly toxic to cats even in low cutaneous applications.

name: Eucalyptus Oil
symptoms: Drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, weakness.
notes: Do not apply directly to pet skin or coats.

name: Antifreeze (Propylene Glycol)
symptoms: Depression, loss of coordination, seizures, Heinz body anemia in cats.
notes: Safer than ethylene glycol, but still highly toxic to pets in quantity.

name: Rat Poison (Bromethalin)
symptoms: Tremors, seizures, coordination loss, hind limb paralysis, death.
notes: Causes cerebral edema (brain swelling). No direct antidote.

name: Metaldehyde (Slug Pellets)
symptoms: Twitching, extreme panting, high fever, seizures.
notes: Pets must be treated immediately with muscle relaxants and cooling therapy.

name: Pennies (Zinc)
symptoms: Vomiting, depression, pale/yellow gums, dark red urine.
notes: Ingested pennies dissolve in stomach acids releasing toxic zinc doses.

name: Baking Soda
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, muscle spasms, congestive heart failure.
notes: In large quantities, disrupts electrolyte balances.

name: Baking Powder
symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, muscle spasms, heart failure.
notes: Contains baking soda and acid salts. Toxic in large quantities.

name: Macadamia Nut Butter
symptoms: Weakness, tremors, vomiting, joint pain.
notes: Avoid cookies or spreads containing macadamia nuts.

name: Play-Dough (Homemade)
symptoms: Vomiting, seizures, extreme thirst, lethargy, death.
notes: Contains very high salt concentrations, causing sodium poisoning.

name: Wild Mushrooms (Gyromitra)
symptoms: Vomiting, bloody diarrhea, liver failure, seizures.
notes: Contains hydrazines. Highly toxic.

name: Wild Mushrooms (Inocybe)
symptoms: Drooling, tear production, tiny pupils, low heart rate, breathing difficulty.
notes: Contains muscarine. Causes severe cholinergic symptoms.
